channel.title: World
channel.description: The Washington Post World section provides information and analysis of breaking world news stories. In addition to our world news and video, Post World News offers discussions and blogs on major international news and economic issues.

item.title: Hegseth, Caine appear before Senate as Iran war escalates
item.dc:creator: Noah Robertson, Ellen Francis, Victoria Craw
item.description: Lawmakers are expected to press for clarity on the Trump administration's strategy for the conflict as it seeks $67 billion in additional funding to cover related costs.

item.title: Trump pledges support for Lebanon peace efforts with Israel as clashes continue
item.dc:creator: Michael Birnbaum
item.description: The president met with his Lebanese counterpart, who is trying to restore government control over southern territory where Israel has been fighting Hezbollah.

item.title: Nearly 100 U.S. troops injured in last two weeks of Iran war, Pentagon says
item.dc:creator: Dan Lamothe, Joshua Yang, Mary Ilyushina
item.description: An intensifying cycle of violence has left at least three U.S. service members dead since the resumption of hostilities a week ago.

item.title: A U.K. prime minister takes charge promising to empower local officials
item.dc:creator: Steve Hendrix
item.description: Andy Burnham, until recently the mayor of Greater Manchester, promised to create a “circuit breaker” moment in the cycle of failure and cynicism that have plagued British politics for the past decade.

item.title: Ukraine bombs warehouses, targeting Wildberries — Russia’s version of Amazon
item.dc:creator: Mary Ilyushina, Natalia Abbakumova
item.description: Ukraine’s long-range drone attacks, including strikes this weekend on e-commerce warehouses, have undercut the Kremlin’s efforts to shield most Russian citizens from the war.
